description:
Returns the current status of a video generation job. When the job completes
successfully the response contains the generated video URL. When the job fails
the response contains a structured `error` object with a machine-readable
`code` and a human-readable `message`.

Both successful and failed completions return HTTP 200 — use the `status`
field (`"done"` or `"failed"`) to distinguish between the two.

parameters.description: The deferred request id returned by a previous video generation request.
parameters.name: request_id

200.description: Video generation completed or failed. Check the `status` field to determine the outcome.

400.description: Bad request. The request is invalid or an invalid API key is provided.

404.description: Not found. No deferred video could be found with the given request_id.

summary: Get the result of a deferred video generation request.

oneOf.description:
Structured error describing why video generation failed.
Only present when the generation encountered a failure (4xx or 5xx).

model.description: The model used to generate the video. Omitted when status is "failed".

progress.description:
Approximate completion percentage for the video generation task (0-100).
- When status is "pending": progress is between 0-99, indicating current completion.
- When status is "done": progress is 100.
- When status is "failed": progress is 0.

status.description: Status of the video generation: "done" when the video is ready.

oneOf.description: Billing and cost information for this request.
The generated video data. Omitted when status is "failed".

VideoError.description: Structured error returned when video generation fails.

code.description:
Machine-readable error code indicating the type of failure.
See `VideoErrorCode` for all possible values.

message.description: Human-readable error message describing the failure.

MediaUsage.description: Billing and cost information for media generation requests (image and video).

cost_in_usd_ticks.description:
The cost of this request expressed in USD ticks.
One USD cent equals 100,000,000 ticks, so one US dollar equals
10,000,000,000 ticks.

duration.description: Duration of the generated video in seconds.

respect_moderation.description:
Whether the video generated by the model respects moderation rules.
The field will be true if the video respects moderation rules. Otherwise
the field will be false and the video url field will be empty.

url.description: A url to the generated video.

VideoErrorCode.description:
Machine-readable error codes for video generation failures.

These are the codes that can appear in `VideoError.code` when polling a
deferred video generation result. Authentication errors, model-not-found
errors, and rate-limit errors are returned synchronously as HTTP errors
and never appear in `VideoError`.

Serializes to/from snake_case strings (e.g. `"invalid_argument"`,
`"internal_error"`) for JSON compatibility.
